steps.title: Identify the chemical signature
steps.content: The test found no iron but high carbon in the pigment. Since ocher contains iron oxide and plant-based pigments contain high carbon, this chemical data directly reveals what type of pigment was used.

steps.title: Apply the chemical evidence
steps.content:
The no-iron, high-carbon result means the beads were decorated with plant-based pigments rather than ocher.

Choices A, C, and D go beyond what the test shows by claiming oldest examples, inventing preferences, or fabricating reasons about ocher availability.

That matches choice B — B is correct.
